bands.bio: Formed in Sacramento in 1988, Deftones fused the aggression of metal with dreamy, shoegaze-influenced atmospherics, becoming one of the most sonically distinct bands in the alternative metal scene.
bands.history: Formed in Sacramento in 1988, Deftones revolutionized alternative metal by fusing heavy riffs with dreamy, shoegaze-influenced atmospherics. Over three decades, they've evolved from a regional nu-metal act into one of the most respected and influential bands in heavy music, known for their meticulous production and emotional depth.
achievements: Platinum certifications for White Pony and Diamond Eyes
Influenced countless metal and alternative bands

bands.bio: Formed in Glendale, California in 1994, System of a Down blended thrash riffs, Armenian folk influences, and politically charged, often satirical lyrics into a wholly unique sound.
bands.history: Formed in Glendale, California in 1994, System of a Down blended thrash metal riffs, Armenian folk influences, and politically charged, often satirical lyrics into a wholly unique sound. The band became one of the most commercially successful and critically acclaimed metal bands of the 2000s, known for their activism and social commentary.
achievements: One of the most-streamed metal bands globally

bands.bio: Formed in Los Angeles in 1990, Tool is known for their intricate, odd-metered compositions, philosophical lyrical themes, and a famously meticulous approach to visual and sonic art.
bands.history: Formed in Los Angeles in 1990, Tool is known for their intricate, odd-metered compositions, philosophical lyrical themes, and a famously meticulous approach to visual and sonic art. The band's perfectionism has resulted in some of the most critically acclaimed albums in metal history, with each release representing years of careful craftsmanship.
achievements: Won Grammy Award for Best Metal Performance

bands.bio: Formed in Des Moines, Iowa in 1995, Slipknot is known for their nine-member lineup, masked stage personas, and brutally heavy, chaotic sound that redefined extreme metal for a new generation.
bands.history: Formed in Des Moines, Iowa in 1995, Slipknot redefined extreme metal with their nine-member lineup, masked stage personas, and brutally heavy, chaotic sound. The band became the gateway to heavy music for millions, known for their intense live performances and evolving production style that balances brutality with melody.
achievements: Evolved from shock metal to sophisticated composition
Influenced a generation of metal bands

bands.bio: Formed in Los Angeles in 1991, Rage Against the Machine fused hip-hop cadences with thrash-heavy riffs and unapologetically radical politics, becoming one of the most influential and confrontational bands of the 90s.
bands.history: Formed in Los Angeles in 1991, Rage Against the Machine fused hip-hop cadences with thrash-heavy riffs and unapologetically radical politics. Though active for only 8 years before initially disbanding, their cultural impact was massive, serving as a template for politically engaged heavy music and protest art.
achievements: Broke barriers between rap and metal genres
Political activism became part of their brand
Reunited multiple times for benefit concerts

bands.bio: Formed in Bakersfield, California in 1993, Korn pioneered the nu metal sound with detuned seven-string riffs, raw emotional lyrics, and a bass-and-groove-driven heaviness that reshaped heavy music in the late 90s.
bands.history: Formed in Bakersfield, California in 1993, Korn pioneered the nu metal sound with detuned seven-string riffs, raw emotional lyrics, and a bass-and-groove-driven heaviness that reshaped heavy music in the late 90s. Jonathan Davis's emotional vulnerability combined with Fieldy's innovative bass work created a sonic blueprint that countless bands would follow.
achievements: Pioneers of the nu metal movement
Influenced the sound of extreme metal
Jonathan Davis became an icon of 90s metal
